rulings.Case Title: ISMAEL BASSO, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. WILIAM KASEL, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Charging Judgment Debtor'S Membership Interest In Wm Kasel Holdings Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, August 28, 2024, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ISMAEL BASSO's Motion For An Order Charging Judgment Debtor'S Membership Interest In Wm Kasel Holdings Llc. Plaintiff's unopposed motion for order charging judgment debtor's membership is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: COURTNEY ELISE STONE VS. MARRIOT INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-613280 WITH CASE CGC-23-607181
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, August 28, 2024, Line 3. DEFENDANT KSSF ENTERPRISES LTD AND W HOTEL MANAGEMENT, INC.'s MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-613280 WITH CASE CGC-23-607181. Defendants' motion to consolidate case numbers CGC 23-607181 and 24-613280 for all purposes is granted; the first-filed shall be the lead action. The actions involve personal injuries allegedly caused by breakage of glass shower doors manufactured by the same defendant and installed by the same defendant at the same hotel - accidents that occurred in the same few weeks. Thus, the actions involve common questions of law and fact. (CCP 1048.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: VONNETTE STONE ET AL VS. ZACCHAEUS IRVING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss, Or, Alternatively, Stay, Due To Inconvenient Forum
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, August 28, 2024, Line 7. 1 - DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC AND RASIER-CA, LLC's Motion To Dismiss, Or, Alternatively, Stay, Due To Inconvenient Forum. Defendants' "motion to dismissdue to inconvenient forum" is granted. This case involves a traffic collision in Mississippi allegedly caused by a Mississippi Uber driver. It is undisputed that everyone involved in the accident and its aftermath (drivers, passengers, law enforcement, medical providers) resided in Mississippi. The Uber entities are headquartered in San Francisco, Kia America in Orange County; negligence and product liability are pled. "Plaintiffs admit that Mississippi is a suitable alternative forum" (Opp. 1:20), but argue convenience factors. First, plaintiffs say the Mississippi courts are "overburdened," but offer no support for that claim. (Opp. 8:25, 9:19.) Second, plaintiffs say California is interested in regulating corporate conduct in the state, but ignores Mississippi's interest in enforcing its own particular ride-share laws. (Id. at 9:2-3.) Third, while conceding that treating doctors, eyewitnesses and material documents are in Mississippi (id. at 10:13-17), plaintiffs say Uber and Kia documents are in California. However, plaintiffs also concede most documents today are "available electronically." (Id. at 2:4.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
